The asteroid belt contains billions of asteroids, yet their combined mass is only about 4% of Earth's Moon.
Light from the Sun takes 8 minutes and 20 seconds to reach Earth, but over 5 hours to reach Pluto.
One day on Venus (rotation) is longer than one Venusian year (orbit around the Sun).
Jupiter's magnetic field is 14 times stronger than Earth's, trapping lethal radiation belts.
Saturn's density is so low that it would float in water, if there were a tub large enough to hold it.
Voyager 1, launched in 1977, is currently over 24 billion kilometers from Earth, traveling in interstellar space.
All of Uranus's moons are named after characters created by William Shakespeare and Alexander Pope.
Neptune's moon Triton orbits in a direction opposite to the planet's rotation—the only large moon in the Solar System to do so.
Mercury contains water ice inside permanently shadowed craters at its poles, despite being closest to the Sun.
Mars is red because its surface is covered in iron oxide (rust), creating its dusty red hue.
